data.body:
=== EXPERIMENT PROMPT (run in a fresh session) ===

Run a grounding experiment for the rebar plan-review gate we designed. Full design context is in rebar session
log `63cc-ec94-4792-464b` — run `rebar show 63cc-ec94-4792-464b` and read the entry titled "DESIGN OF RECORD:
plan-of-record-v4.md"; §5 is the criteria registry, §4 is the feedback-authoring spec, §6 is the
chunking/budget/proportionate-scrutiny model. The epic is `5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa`.

GOAL: ground two defaults in real observed outcomes — (1) the CHUNKING strategy (how many Layer-2 review criteria
to batch per LLM call before result quality degrades) and (2) the BUDGET posture (cost + latency distribution per
review, to set a generous-but-grounded cap and a sense of where the INDETERMINATE/budget-cap outcome would
trigger). You are authorized to run ~100 experiment runs via subagents (a Workflow or subagent fan-out is fine).
This is a SIMULATION of the not-yet-built gate: a reviewer subagent applies a CHUNK of our Layer-2 advisory
criteria to a ticket's plan and returns structured findings authored to the §4 feedback spec (grounded in a
criterion/file/section, specific + actionable, severity-ranked, checklist-form).

INPUTS:
- Real plans from the DSO ticket store, READ-ONLY, at ~/digital-service-orchestra (an event-sourced rebar-like
  store: orphan `tickets` git branch and/or `.tickets-tracker/<ticket-id>/.cache.json`; a ticket's `description`
  + `acceptance_criteria`/comments are its "plan"). Select a STRATIFIED sample of ~5-6 real tickets spanning
  complexity — trivial task, moderate task/story, complex / epic-with-children — using size signals (AC count,
  children count, description length; note DSO `file_impact` is empty so don't rely on it). Cite the real ticket
  ids you pick.
- Our own epic `5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa` (`rebar show`) as a DOGFOOD target — review it with our criteria too.

METHOD (≈100 runs):
- Apply only the LAYER-2 advisory/judgment criteria (chunking is about how many of those batch per LLM call; the
  Layer-1 deterministic checks are tool-based and out of scope for this experiment).
- Vary CHUNK SIZE across {1, 3, 6, ALL} criteria per reviewer call.
- For each (ticket × chunk size) run the reviewer subagent and REPEAT 5× to measure non-determinism.
  ~5 tickets × 4 chunk sizes × 5 repeats = 100 runs (adjust to land near 100; include the epic as one target).
- Use chunk-size = 1 (one criterion per call) as the QUALITY BASELINE. Do NOT tell the reviewer subagent that
  chunk size is the variable.

MEASURE per run: the findings (criterion, location, severity), token cost, wall-clock. Then COMPARE each coarser
chunk against the per-criterion baseline for the same ticket:
  (a) RECALL — does batching cause the reviewer to drop/dilute criteria the baseline catches?
  (b) NOISE — does it add spurious/duplicate findings?
  (c) CONSISTENCY — variance across the 5 repeats (non-determinism).
The chunk size at which recall drops or noise/variance rises materially vs the baseline = the degradation point;
the recommended default is the LARGEST chunk before that.

DELIVER (append to session log `63cc-ec94-4792-464b` AND save to a file):
  1. The observed quality-degradation point + a recommended DEFAULT chunk size (and whether it varies by ticket complexity).
  2. The cost + latency distribution (p50/p95) per review and per complexity tier -> a recommended GENEROUS budget
     cap (comfortably covers ~p95 + headroom, per the "generous to gather data" posture) and where INDETERMINATE would trigger.
  3. The DOGFOOD review of epic `5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa` — what our criteria flag about it (a real data point + a
     check on both the criteria and the epic itself; expect it may flag "decompose into children").
  4. Honest caveats: DSO is ONE ticket-creation style, not representative of rebar's diverse client base, and this
     SIMULATES the system rather than running the real one — so treat outputs as grounded starting defaults, not final.

CONSTRAINTS: do NOT mutate the DSO ticket store (read-only). The reviewer subagent must be a different instance
than any that authored a plan. After this ~100-run round, stop and report; we'll decide whether to run a second
validation round.
